title: ComfyUI Tutorial Series Ep 35: How to Run ComfyUI in the Cloud
description:
In this tutorial, learn how to run ComfyUI in the cloud—a great option if you don’t have a powerful PC for running AI models like Flux. I’ll walk you through a platform called Running Hub, which lets you use ComfyUI online without needing high-end hardware.

Why Use ComfyUI in the Cloud?
✅ No need for a high-end GPU
✅ Faster processing times
✅ Saves storage space
✅ Accessible from any device

I’ll guide you through setting up an account, uploading workflows, and running AI models step by step. Plus, you’ll get 1,000 free coins using the special link in the description—no referral commissions, just a bonus for my viewers! 

You can use this link when you join for the first time to get 1000 free coins. Also, if you click on the "Workflows" tab, you can see my cloud workflows.
https://www.runninghub.ai/user-center/1859171522652098561?utm_source=kol02-RH003

Get the workflows for all my episodes and instructions from discord for free
https://discord.gg/gggpkVgBf3

Check Other Episodes
http
transcript: Welcome to episode 35 of our comfy UI tutorial series.  Today I will show you how to run comfy UI in the cloud.  Many of you have told me that you don't have a good PC to run Flux or some other powerful models  and you're looking for an alternative to run comfy UI online.  I have a good video card so I don't really need the cloud.  But when I'm not at my office I do miss accessing my workflows and running models like Flux.  I've tried a few options I found that allow you to run comfy UI  and I discovered one that is more affordable and doesn't cost money while I upload models,  create workflows and so on. It only costs me when I actually run the workflows.  So the advantages are no need for a powerful PC, faster processing,  save storage space because all the models are on the cloud, not on your computer, runs anywhere.  You just log in from your device. The disadvantages are it may cost money but as you will see  you get a few credits for free each day. You need an internet connection to run it.  Some new or lesser known nodes might not be available there.  The platform I will be using is called Running Hub and I will add a special link in the description  for my followers so you can get 1000 coins to test it for free.  I don't get any money if you use the link, you just get extra coins.  You simply click on the sign-in button and you can sign in with Google.  Once you log in click on your username to see how many coins you have,  subscribe and adjust settings. The interface is quite simple.  You have the AI apps which are easy to use apps made from a comfy UI workflows.  And then we have comfy UI workflows. I'm not referring to workflows for download but to  tested workflows that you can run directly with no errors or problems.  You also have access to many models that you can use in your workflows or upload your own.  The workspace is where you can build your own workflows and in the user center you can find all  your generations like images, audio or videos. Under publications you can find all the apps  and workflows you've made public. I've only made workflows public so people can see each node I  used and the settings I applied. If I don't want people to see my workflow I either keep it private  and don't publish it publicly or I publish it as an app. So people only see an easy to use  interface for that workflow. For models you can find the checkpoints and Laura's you've uploaded.  There's also an API call tab where you can find more information. Now let's open a workflow I  created. If you're on my profile and click on workflows you can run this workflow as well since  it's public or if you click on your user center you can see your published workflows. I created a few  workflows you can try out a few flux models one for audio generation some upscalers and so on.  Each user has an ID this is mine. You can also search for workflows created by other users let's say  I want to run this workflow. You can see here some previews I added for this workflow like the types  of images it can generate along with a short description on the right. You have node information to  see how many nodes it has. You can download the workflow if you want to run it locally just install  any missing nodes and models. You can also add it to your favorites or of course you can run it  on the cloud since that's the purpose of this video. As you can see all the workflows will run on  the RTX 4000 and 90 video card. The workflow will load and depending on how many nodes it has  it may take a few seconds. Once it's loaded you'll have access to the workflow. Here you can change  settings save it and everything is private. As you can see so far no payment is needed compared to  other platforms where you pay when building or opening a workflow. Here it only costs coins when  you actually run it. To run it click on the run button and the timer will start. It will stop  automatically when the workflow finishes the generation. If you want to stop it sooner you can  click the cancel button to stop the generation. But I want to run it so I'll press the run button  again. This workflow uses a flux model and a Laura model so it will probably take around a minute to  run. The first time you run it it takes longer and costs more coins. The second time is faster  and costs fewer coins and here's the result. A nice illustration generated with comfy UI online.  You can generate another one and continue. You have access to many models and a lot of custom  nodes are already installed. Not all but most popular ones are already there. As you can see the  second time it only costs five coins and you can get 100 free coins each day to play with it.  If you use the link I provided you get a one time 1000 coins. Alternatively you can subscribe to  get more. From here you can go back and save the workflow if you want. So now I'm back in my workspace.  To get to the workspace you can also just click on the create button and it will bring you here.  On this screen you can import a comfy UI workflow or open some ready made ones to get started  like text to image or image to image. Then you can modify them to fit your needs. You can also  create a blank workflow and practice using tutorials I have on YouTube. The only difference is  the model names. Some may have different names. Let's click import and I'll show you how to  import your own workflows. Let's say I open a workflow I created in episode 10 like the simple  text to image flux workflow. I think it has to be under 50 characters. Here you have all the workflows you've  uploaded and you just click on the name to open it. Wait for it to load and then if you didn't get any  red nodes or errors that means all the nodes are already available on the platform and are installed.  You only need to select the right models. As you can see if you want to make a workflow public  you need to run it first that way. They make sure the workflows run when you try to run workflows  from other people. Add your prompt, size and settings and run the workflow. We got an error but  don't worry. Let's read what it says. If you click on the Laura name a new  window will open where you can select the model. As you can see in the top right it shows which  base model was used for that Laura. Then add a prompt that describes what you want to do and put that word in quotes. You can adjust  the Control Net strength and the number of steps if needed. For the steps if you get blurry images  try using 30 steps instead of 20. After adjusting everything click Run and it will start running.  You can rename the workflows and save them and you can also enable auto save if you want.  There are more icons like models.
